title: Us Coffee Chain
description: Coffee Chain is a cafeteria that sells different product types namely coffee, espresso, herbal tea, and tea in 4 markets covering 20 states. Coffee Chain is engaged in selling 13 products under 4 product types. The report entails an understanding of the business and data of the coffee chain. The final outcome is to find the problems in the profitability of the products and the factors which contributed to the issues. This reporting and analysis are being done using Power BI Software. I have used various functionalities offered by Tableau Software to gain insights into the coffee chain. I proceeded with high-level analysis and then investigated further using methods like drill-down, filtering, hierarchy, calculated Measures,  etc. I have calculated different measures like Sales Per Month, YOY%, GPM%,  GPM YOY%, etc. to analyze the profitability of the chain stores. The report tells us about the products which are underperforming and the causes and factors for the underperformance.
stepByStepContent:
Us Coffee Chain
Coffee Chain is a cafeteria that sells different product types namely coffee, espresso, herbal tea, and tea in 4 markets covering 20 states. Coffee Chain is engaged in selling 13 products under 4 product types. The report entails an understanding of the business and data of the coffee chain. The final outcome is to find the problems in the profitability of the products and the factors which contributed to the issues. This reporting and analysis are being done using Power BI Software. I have used various functionalities offered by Tableau Software to gain insights into the coffee chain. I proceeded with high-level analysis and then investigated further using methods like drill-down, filtering, hierarchy, calculated Measures, etc. I have calculated different measures like Sales Per Month, YOY%, GPM%, GPM YOY%, etc. to analyze the profitability of the chain stores. The report tells us about the products which are underperforming and the causes and factors for the underperformance.
Step Name:Visual 4. Line and Stacked column Visual
Detailed Instructions:

Visual 4: Line and Stacked column Visual
 
1. Copy visual 3 and Paste 
2. Change the Shared Axis to Date; Year/ Month from the calendar table that we can drill through.
 
3. Give visual tile; Sales by Month
 
Use the following step-by-step Gif to create the visual

Insight: 

Line and Stacked Column Chart in Power BI

Step Name:Load Excel file into Power BI Desktop
Detailed Instructions:
 Dataset Link:
 https://drive.google.com/file/d/1O7u9EHrZhl66ngKRmcTa636V64UlkKGR/view

 Open Power BI Desktop2.        
 Click Get data on Power BI Desktop 
  Under GetData select Excel
  The select coffee chain, open it and select the fact, location, and Product tables.
Click on the load button to load the data, Use the following step-by-step Gif 

Insight: 

How to Load Excel File into Power BI Desktop

Us Coffee Chain
Coffee Chain is a cafeteria that sells different product types namely coffee, espresso, herbal tea, and tea in 4 markets covering 20 states. Coffee Chain is engaged in selling 13 products under 4 product types. The report entails an understanding of the business and data of the coffee chain. The final outcome is to find the problems in the profitability of the products and the factors which contributed to the issues. This reporting and analysis are being done using Power BI Software. I have used various functionalities offered by Tableau Software to gain insights into the coffee chain. I proceeded with high-level analysis and then investigated further using methods like drill-down, filtering, hierarchy, calculated Measures, etc. I have calculated different measures like Sales Per Month, YOY%, GPM%, GPM YOY%, etc. to analyze the profitability of the chain stores. The report tells us about the products which are underperforming and the causes and factors for the underperformance.
Step Name:UNDERSTANDING THE DATA
Detailed Instructions:

1. In order to start the analysis of the data and data mining, It is necessary to understand the data from a business perspective. 
2. Here, the analysis is done on the data of the coffee source to see if any products are creating profits or losses. 
3. Hence, the analysis starts with understanding whether are there any products underperforming, are coffee houses in certain states, not making profits, the Reasons for the loss statewide, and the correlation to profit. Data Understanding. The fact Table datasheet that is exported, lists all the sales transactions. Each transaction shows us the area code or transaction location. The location datasheet tells us which state each area code is found in. It also tells what market each area code is located in and its market size. The Product table shows the product type and product it product by product ID, This Three sets of data give us more information to analyze to find the profitability issues of the coffee house chain.

Insight: 

Understanding Data In PowerBI

Use the following to create the date table:
Calendar Dax
Calendar = CALENDAR(DATE(2013,1,1),DATE(2016,12,31))
Use the following step-by-step Gif

Power BI for Beginners: How to create a Date Table in Power BI

Step Name:Data Modeling
Detailed Instructions:

 

To set the relationship, you must drag a line between the common columns. You can also view the “Relationship” in a data model in Power BI. In the Relationship tab, you can see the relationship between data sources. When you add multiple data sources to Power BI visualization, the tool automatically tries to detect the relationship between the columns. When you navigate to the Relationship tab, you can view the relationship. In this step, you will create a relationship between Fact Table and Calendar. We will drag and drop the date column from the calendar table to the Order date column in the Fact table

 Follow the following Gif to create the relationship:

Insight: 

Create and manage relationships in Power BI Desktop

Us Coffee Chain
Coffee Chain is a cafeteria that sells different product types namely coffee, espresso, herbal tea, and tea in 4 markets covering 20 states. Coffee Chain is engaged in selling 13 products under 4 product types. The report entails an understanding of the business and data of the coffee chain. The final outcome is to find the problems in the profitability of the products and the factors which contributed to the issues. This reporting and analysis are being done using Power BI Software. I have used various functionalities offered by Tableau Software to gain insights into the coffee chain. I proceeded with high-level analysis and then investigated further using methods like drill-down, filtering, hierarchy, calculated Measures, etc. I have calculated different measures like Sales Per Month, YOY%, GPM%, GPM YOY%, etc. to analyze the profitability of the chain stores. The report tells us about the products which are underperforming and the causes and factors for the underperformance.
Step Name:Creating DAX Measures
Detailed Instructions:

 To Create a DAX Measure follow these steps:
 
1.       Select the table “ Fact”
 
2.       Right Click and select New Measure
 
3.       Write the following DAX Measures on the space provide one at a time and click the right mark
 
4.       Total  COGS = sum(Fact[Budget Sales])
5.       Total Profit = [.Total Sales]-[.Total COGS]
6.       Total Sales = sum(Fact[Sales])
7.       Total sales LY = CALCULATE([.Total Sales],DATEADD('Calander'[Date].[Date],-1,YEAR))
8.       GPM % = DIVIDE([.Total Profit],[.Total Sales],0)
9.       GPM LY = CALCULATE([GPM %],DATEADD('Calander'[Date].[Date],-1,YEAR))
10.   GPM YOY% = var gpmly = CALCULATE([GPM %],DATEADD('Calander'[Date].[Date],-1,YEAR)) Return DIVIDE(([GPM %]-[GPM LY]),[GPM LY])+0
11.   Sales Per Month = DIVIDE([.Total Sales],DISTINCTCOUNT('Calander'[Date].[Month]))
12.   YOY% = Var TotalsalesLY = CALCULATE([.Total Sales],DATEADD('Calander'[Date].[Date],-1,YEAR)) Return DIVIDE([.Total Sales]-[.Total sales LY],[.Total sales LY])+0
13.   Arrow GPM YOY% = SWITCH(TRUE(),[GPM YOY%]<0,UNICHAR(9660),UNICHAR(9650))
14.   Color Arrow GPM YOY% = IF([Arrow GPM YOY%] = UNICHAR(9660), "Green", "Red")
15.   Arrow YOY% = SWITCH(TRUE(),[YOY%]<0,UNICHAR(9660),UNICHAR(9650))
16.   Color Arrow YOY% = IF([Arrow YOY%] = UNICHAR(9660), "Green", "Red")

Insight: 

 DAX in Power BI

Step Name:Creating Refreshed Date
Detailed Instructions:

1.       Open the Power BI desktop, and click on the Transform Data option
 
2.        Under the Home ribbon, click on the New Source option and select Blank Query.
3.         go to the formula bar, write below the M query, and press enter.
        = DateTime.LocalNow() , then  On table Query1 in place under the Queries section,    right-click on it and rename it to Last Refreshed Date.     
 4.       Now we will convert this date data into a table format. For this, we need to right-click on the To Table tab and select the To Table Option. When you click on the To Table option, you will see it is converted into a single column.
5.          Now, Change the Data type to Date/Time, and to save all those changes, click on the Close & Apply tab.

Insight: 

How to Add the Last Refreshed Date and Time to a Power BI Report

Step Name:Adding Company Logo, and Project title.
Detailed Instructions:

For  company logo 
 
1. Click on Insert from the ribbon.
2. Then click on Image in Elements
3. Select the Image from the file

Adding Project Title 
 
1. Click on the Text box from Home or insert the ribbon.
 
2. Begin typing the name of the project (US Coffee Chain)
3.  background Color to #6B2328
 
4. Select the text then change the font size to 32 and font type to Segoe UI and Bold

Insight: 

How to Put Logo or Image in Power BI Desktop

How to put Text Box for Title Header in Power BI Desktop

portfolioContent.title: Us Coffee Chain
portfolioContent.portfolioSummary: Analyzed profitability and performance of a coffee chain's products across multiple markets using Power BI and Tableau.
portfolioContent.summary: This project involved analyzing sales and profitability data for a US coffee chain selling 13 products across 4 product types in 20 states. Using Power BI and Tableau, various measures such as Sales Per Month, YOY%, and Gross Profit Margin were calculated to identify underperforming products and contributing factors. The analysis included data modeling, DAX measures, and interactive visualizations to deliver actionable business insights.
portfolioContent.businessProblem: The coffee chain faced challenges in identifying which products were underperforming and understanding the factors impacting profitability. Without clear insights, optimizing product offerings and improving margins was difficult. The project aimed to uncover these issues through detailed data analysis.
objectives: Analyze sales trends and gross profit margins over time
Provide actionable insights to improve product performance
workflow: Load and understand sales data from Excel into Power BI
Create calendar table and establish data relationships
Develop DAX measures for sales and profitability metrics
Build interactive visualizations with drill-down and filtering
Analyze results to identify underperforming products and causes
keyInsights: Certain products under specific product types showed consistent underperformance
Sales and gross profit margins varied significantly across the 4 markets
Year-over-year sales trends revealed declining performance in some product categories
Profitability issues were linked to specific products and market segments
businessImpact: Enabled targeted strategies to improve profitability of underperforming products
Provided management with clear visibility into sales trends and margins
Supported data-driven decision making for product and market optimization
